states.name: Ryouma
states.title: Warrior of the Nameless Beast
states.description:
Those who carry the royal blood of Sanga inherit two vows: a vow to protect their homeland, and a vow to guard the shrine of the Death Primordial.

Since birth, Ryouma has borne these vows, not that anyone knows. As a bastard, his blood is as much a secret as the stone door beneath the shogun's palace, the gateway between the world of the living and the dead. His mother raised him to live as a shadow, silently slipping along behind those in the light.

Perhaps, if he had inherited his father's wisdom instead of his pride, he could have obeyed her. But Ryouma was born a warrior through and through, and thus he sought one day to hunt the dragon with no name.

states.name: Ryouma
states.title: Warrior of the Nameless Beast
states.description:
The ancient dragons of Erden bore grand names: Fornaxos, Kaidaros, Altaireon, Diabolos. All were silenced when the Goddess came, never to be spoken again. Only the dragon who slithered between the stars survived to see the new world.

Ryouma located this beast in his mountain lair, determined to prove his blade could best a dragon. He gave the beast a good fight, but in the end, could not overcome his nimble grace and speed. Satisfied with his effort, he submitted himself to a warrior's death, certain no one he left behind would miss him.

The dragon, having glimpsed the young warrior's true potential, refused to kill him, and instead made him his apprentice.

states.name: Ryouma
states.title: Warrior of the Nameless Beast
states.description:
When Ryouma mastered the dragon's sword technique, he honored him by sharing his true name at last, then bid him return to his kingdom. At first, the people marveled at his skill, forcing the shogun to honor him with a place among his guard.

But honor breeds resentment, and shadows wither in the sun. When the time came for Ryouma's companions to cover his flank, they abandoned him instead.

All the mastery in the world cannot turn one blade into ten. As ever, he fought valiantly, but this time, the proud dragon warrior met his end.

At least, so the legend goes. Little do the storytellers know that samurai and his nameless dragon live still, preserved from death by powers beyond their control, destined to one day meet again.
